title: intercultural
caption_full:
This coming Tuesday, my musician friends Farhan and Kapil, and I are presenting to you, that is, for all manner of listeners an interactive and enjoyable view of Indian sounds - what they have meant, what they mean and how rhythm is kept and such like.
This is from 11 am to 12 noon at @alliancefrdelhi which is marking 70 years of its functioning in Delhi and enriching its people, particularly through such activities which we may call “intercultural”.
We’ll be explaining stuff to you as friends, in an admixture of Hindi and English and will also propose some fun surprises towards the end, apart from taking audience questions. The more of you, indeed, the merrier it will be for ALL OF US! So, not only should you come but also share this post/the poster with other amigos of yours who take an interest in learning things the fun way!!!

title: प्रातिज्ञा
venue: राजस्थान महाविद्यालय सभागार, जवाहरलाल नेहरू मार्ग, जयपुर, राजस्थान
caption_full:
#प्रातिज्ञा – प्रदर्शन कलाओं के क्षेत्र के दिग्गजों के अनुभवों से संवाद की एक विशेष शृंखला
संगीत नाटक अकादेमी, नई दिल्ली एवं राजस्थान महाविद्यालय, जयपुर (राजस्थान विश्वविद्यालय, जयपुर) के सहयोग से आयोजित ‘प्रातिज्ञा’ के विशेष सत्र में आपका हार्दिक स्वागत है।
प्रतिभागी कलाकार:
• पंडित विश्व मोहन भट्ट
• श्रीमती शशि सांखला
• श्री म. के. रैना
📅 30 जून 2026
🕦 पूर्वाह्न 11:30 बजे से
📍 राजस्थान महाविद्यालय सभागार, जवाहरलाल नेहरू मार्ग, जयपुर, राजस्थान
आइए, इस प्रेरणादायक संवाद का हिस्सा बनें और भारतीय प्रदर्शन कलाओं की समृद्ध परम्पराओं एवं सृजनात्मक अनुभवों से रूबरू हों।

title: Bunkar… Stories Woven Anew
caption_full:
Join us for an evening of theatre; “Bunkar… Stories Woven Anew” (Hindi/ 75 mins) Directed & Performed by Danish Iqbal. Bunkar is an immersive blend of storytelling, theatre, and live music that brings everyday experiences to life through Cycle Kahani by Kunal Singh, explores childhood, friendship, love, and life’s bittersweet moments with warmth, humour, and nostalgia; Hero Ek Love Story by Ravi Bule, a tale of a charming young romantic, lost in daydreams of his neighbour, is jolted back to reality when her shocking revelation shatters his world. Prod. Rangsaaz Theatre Group.
Tickets at Rs.500, Rs.350 and Rs.200 available online on bookmyshow.com and at the Programmes desk, IHC.
Ticket link in bio.
18th July | 5:00pm & 7:30pm
Venue: Stein Auditorium

title: Through Pages Turned
caption_full:
Join us for an evening of dance and music; “Through Pages Turned” (English, Hindi/ 60 mins) Conceptualised & choreographed by Rachana Yadav. Life is all about seeking. What do we seek? How do we seek? Where do we seek? What do we gain and lose in the process of seeking? This piece will travel through this one search till the point of revelation. Prod. The Rachana Yadav Kathak Studio.
Tickets at Rs.500, Rs.350 & Rs.150 are available on bookmyshow.com and at the Habitat Programmes desk.
Ticket link in bio.
19th July | 7:00pm | Stein Auditorium

title: Ticket link in bio.
caption_full:
NT Live Broadcast. The Playboy of the Western World (English/ 140 mins with interval) by John Millington Synge. Dir. Caitríona McLaughlin. Cast: Nicola Coughlan, Éanna Hardwicke, Siobhán McSweeney & others. Pegeen Flaherty’s life is turned upside down when a young man walks into her pub claiming that he’s killed his father. Instead of being shunned, the killer becomes a local hero and begins to win hearts, that is until a second man unexpectedly arrives on the scene.
Tickets at Rs.500, Rs.350 & Rs.250 are available online on bookmyshow.com and at the Habitat Programmes desk.
Ticket link in bio.

title: a solo exhibition of works by Afzal Pathan (1936–2000), one of
venue: Venue: Shridharani Gallery, Triveni Kala Sangam, New Delhi
caption_full:
Afzal Pathan: Ek Bahta Dariya
Raza Foundation, in collaboration with Triveni Kala Sangam, presents a solo exhibition of works by Afzal Pathan (1936–2000), one of the most distinctive voices in Indian abstract art.
Known for his luminous forms, flowing compositions, and remarkable technical mastery, Pathan created a visual language that was both deeply personal and universally resonant. His paintings invite viewers into a world of movement, rhythm, and quiet contemplation.
Curated by Akhilesh, the exhibition offers an opportunity to revisit the legacy of an artist whose work continues to inspire generations of painters and art lovers.
Opening: 30 June 2026, 6:30 PM
Exhibition Dates: 30 June – 10 July 2026
Venue: Shridharani Gallery, Triveni Kala Sangam, New Delhi
Gallery Hours: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM

title: Karwaan Baithak #8 | The Physics of Being Late: a discourse on the Physics and Philosophy of Time, a conversation with Professor Wasi Haider
caption_full:
Karwaan Baithak #8 | The Physics of Being Late: a discourse on the Physics and Philosophy of Time, a conversation with Professor Wasi Haider
On Saturday, 4th July 2026, at 4 PM IST at Arthshila, New Delhi. Registration link in bio!
One of India's distinguished theoretical physicists, Professor Wasi Haider is Professor Emeritus of Physics at Aligarh Muslim University. Over a career spanning five decades, his research has shaped modern nuclear physics, with pioneering contributions to many-body theory, nuclear matter, optical potentials, and quantum theory. He has collaborated with some of the world's leading physicists, including Nobel Laureate Professor Abdus Salam at the International Centre for Theoretical Physics (ICTP), Trieste, and has delivered invited lectures at Oxford, ICTP, Osaka, Kyushu, and several other leading institutions across the world.
In this Baithak, we turn to one of humanity's oldest and most elusive questions: What is time?
We speak of time as though it flows like a river—moving relentlessly from the past to the future. Yet modern physics tells a far stranger story. Einstein showed that time does not pass equally for everyone. Gravity bends it. Motion stretches it. There is no universal "now." At the deepest level of quantum reality, time itself may disappear from the fundamental equations of the universe.
Why, then, do we experience time flowing in only one direction? Is the arrow of time a law of nature—or simply a consequence of entropy? Why does time seem to slow during moments of waiting, yet race through moments of joy? And if time is not fundamental, what does that mean for our understanding of reality itself?
Bringing together physics, philosophy, and the history of scientific thought, Professor Haider will guide us through one of the greatest intellectual revolutions of the modern age—challenging many of our deepest intuitions about the universe.
Registrations are now open.

title: Tragedy Mein Comedy
caption_full:
Join us for an evening of theatre; “Tragedy Mein Comedy” (Hindi/ 80 mins) Wri. & Dir. Varun Sharma. The plot revolves around a murder in Hasija mansion followed by a series of events which questions the authenticity of the situation, the play takes a humorous turn when everyone gets involved and is suspected for the murder. With each proceeding, the case gets more and more complicated and finally gets concluded on an unexpected note. Prod. Theatreleela Acting Studio.
Tickets at Rs.500, Rs.350 & Rs.200 are available on bookmyshow.com and at the Habitat Programmes desk. Ticket link in bio.
11th July | 7:30pm | Stein Auditorium

title: NEO, URBAN, AVATAR: A Mythopoesis of the Contemporary Times-II
caption_full:
Exhibition Alert 🔔
𝐍𝐄𝐎, 𝐔𝐑𝐁𝐀𝐍, 𝐀𝐕𝐀𝐓𝐀𝐑: 𝐀 𝐌𝐲𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐩𝐨𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐓𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐬-𝐈𝐈
𝐀𝐫𝐭 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐬 𝐛𝐲 𝐁𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐫𝐚 𝐏𝐚𝐧𝐢 𝐍𝐄𝐎, 𝐔𝐑𝐁𝐀𝐍, 𝐀𝐕𝐀𝐓𝐀𝐑: 𝐀 𝐌𝐲𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐩𝐨𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐓𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐬-𝐈𝐈
𝐀𝐫𝐭 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐬 𝐛𝐲 𝐁𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐫𝐚 𝐏𝐚𝐧𝐢
The exhibition presents a body of drawing-based paintings that explore the fragmented realities of contemporary urban existence. Rooted in Indian aesthetic traditions yet shaped by a globalized and technologized visual culture, the works weave together mythology, memory, satire, consumerism and environmental traces into an evolving contemporary mythscape.
𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐞𝐱𝐡𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐛𝐞 𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐮𝐠𝐮𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐨𝐧 𝐓𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐝𝐚𝐲, 𝟑𝟎 𝐉𝐮𝐧𝐞’𝟐𝟔 𝐚𝐭 𝟏𝟕:𝟑𝟎 𝐡𝐫𝐬
𝐄𝐱𝐡𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐚𝐥𝐤𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡𝐬 𝐝𝐚𝐢𝐥𝐲 𝐛𝐲 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐬𝐭 𝐚𝐭 𝟏𝟕:𝟑𝟎 𝐡𝐫𝐬
📆 𝟎𝟏 -𝟏𝟒 𝐉𝐮𝐥 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟔,
⏰ 𝟏𝟏:𝟎𝟎 𝐀𝐌-𝟎𝟕:𝟎𝟎 𝐏𝐌
📍𝐀𝐫𝐭 𝐆𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐫𝐲, 𝐊𝐚𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐯𝐢 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐞𝐱, 𝐈𝐈𝐂

title: SIFAT
caption_full:
रूमी, कबीर, सिनेमा और साहित्य  सब एक ही मंच पर!
​फाउंडेशन "SIFAT" की एक बेहतरीन पेशकश !
​स्थान- रंधावा ऑडिटोरियम, पंजाब कला भवन, सेक्टर-16, चंडीगढ़
रविवार, 5 जुलाई 2026 |  सुबह 10:00 बजे से
​ क्या होगा खास?
​महान विद्वान प्रो. मालविंदरजीत सिंह वड़ैच को श्रद्धांजलि।
​मोहन राकेश की मशहूर कहानी 'मवाली' पर आधारित फिल्म की स्क्रीनिंग
​'रूमी मीट्स कबीर' – एक रूहानी महफ़िल जहाँ अश्विन 'अफ़राद' की दास्तानगोई और सावनी शिखरे की आवाज़ में सूफीवाद और भक्ति का संगम होगा।
​ एंट्री फ्री है! (सीटें पहले आओ, पहले पाओ के आधार पर मिलेंगी, इसलिए समय से पहुंचें।)

title: Where Cinema Meets Cultural Heritage.
caption_full:
Where Cinema Meets Cultural Heritage. ✨
The Art Grandeur proudly joins hands with the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA) to celebrate India’s rich artistic legacy at Film Expo India 2026. Together, we bridge cinema and culture, preserving traditions while inspiring creative futures.
📍 Bharat Mandapam, ITPO, New Delhi
